title: MEDLIFE & Pre-Med Club Hosting Dermatologist Dr. Grau
description: MEDLIFE and the Pre-Med Club will host a dermatologist to speak! Come hang out and listen to Dr. Grau talk about her journey to medicine and dermatology. We will have some snacks and drinks!!

description: Indigenous Art Forms is hosting a beading/craft circle in the Five Moons Lounge from 7pm-9pm! Everyone is welcome and there will be free food!! Wednesday, April 15th. 7:00pm-9:00pm. Five Moons Lounge. Copeland Hall.

title: Campus Corner Social
description: Come celebrate our Founder's Day with FREE dinner and chats at a restaurant on campus corner!

title: Medical Spanish Workshop
host: Hispanic American Student Association / HOSA- Future Health Professionals / Kidney Disease Screening and Awareness Program / Latino Medical Student Association + / Pre-Dental Club / Spanish Language Club
location: Jim Thorpe Multicultural Center, Multipurpose Room B and C
description: Join us for our second Medical Spanish Workshop! Learn practical Spanish phrases and engage in real life medical conversations to strengthen your skills. This event is open to all levels of Spanish speakers! Location: Jim Thorpe Multicultural Center, Multipurpose Room B and C Date: April 16th⏰ Time: 6:00–7:30pm Whether you're pre-med, pre-PA, pre-dental, nursing, or simply interested in improving communication with Spanish-speaking patients, this workshop is the perfect place to start. We'll kick off the evening with a welcome dinner at 6:00 PM, followed by guided rotations through hands-on medical Spanish learning stations. Participants will receive a "medical passport" to collect stamps at each station. Students will rotate through stations focused on real healthcare communication scenarios, including: Physical Exam – Learn common instructions used during check-ups Clinical Interview – Practice taking patient histories in Spanish Medical Terminology – Build essential vocabulary for symptoms and conditions Intake Forms – Practice completing real clinical forms in Spanish Medication Instructions – Learn how to explain prescriptions and treatment plans⚖️ Medical Decision-Making – Explore conversations about patient preferences and advance directives With a rapidly growing Hispanic population and many Spanish-speaking patients seeking care in community and student-led clinics, even basic medical Spanish is an invaluable skill for future healthcare professionals. Come connect with members from HOSA-Future Health Professionals, KDSAP, LMSA, HASA, Pre-Dental Club and the OU Spanish Language Club, and learn about ways to get involved at OU. Free food and drinks will be provided!

title: AIAA April GBM: Movie Night
host: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
location: Willoughby Lounge, 4th floor of Felgar Hall
description: Join AIAA for our April GBM ‼️‼️ We’ll be having a MOVIE NIGHT with a screening of “La La Land in Space” ; we’ll also go over what AIAA has in store for the final stretch of the semester FREE SNACKS WILL BE PROVIDED ‼️‼️ We’re excited to see you there!

title: The Big Event 2026 Alternative Date (April 18th, 2026)
description: The Big Event is the University of Oklahoma’s official day of community service! On one Saturday in the spring, over 6,000 volunteers--students, faculty, and staff--come together to give back to the great Norman/OKC community that we call home throughout the school year. Making an impact on the community members and organizations that support our university in many ways makes The Big Event a genuinely fulfilling and memorable experience each year! Join us for the 27th annual Big Event on Saturday, April 18th, and get the chance to serve the community with your friends or organization! As a volunteer, you will begin your day at 8 am at the North Oval for our grand Opening Ceremony with free breakfast, goodies, hype from the OU spirit squad, and so much more! Following that, volunteering will take place from 10 AM to 2 PM at your assigned job site, where you will participate in various activities, such as painting, gardening, organizing, community interaction, etc. Don’t miss out on this spectacular opportunity! If you have any questions regarding volunteer registration or need any accommodations, please email bigevent@ou.edu.

title: Kolor Love Pool Party
host: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated / Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity Inc.
description: This annual event is proudly hosted by the members of the Kappa Alpha Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. and the Zeta Omega Chapter of Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc. as we celebrate our charter day. Join us for an exciting pool party featuring great food, refreshing drinks, and music that will keep the energy high all day long. Come out, relax, and enjoy a fun-filled day with great vibes and even better company!

title: Make Your Own Travel Bags with Cross Community Council
description: Come over to the Cross A lobby for free food (pizza) and crafts(tote bag decorating)!! Create the perfect tote bag with tons of fun travel necessities!

title: Earth Day Fair
description: Are you ready to celebrate 56 years of Earth Day? Join Green Week on the South Oval in celebration of 56 years of Earth Day! We will have different organizations from across campus tabling, food, ice cream, and yard games. Make sure to stop by to see what Green Week's all about!

title: Tulip Farm Workday
description: Join us to help Amy over at Sweet Pea Farm in Norman, Oklahoma! We will be helping tend her tulip garden to ensure her local farm prospers. Snacks and drinks will be provided for all attendees.

title: Popcorn Thursday
description: Enjoy free popcorn from Student Life!

title: GeoCareers Day
description: Come hear from Dr. Lena Cole and Andrew Oordt on their career journeys in paleontology and environmental work, respectively! Are you curious about geology-oriented career options after graduation? This event is for you. FREE lunch will be provided as well as a networking opportunity with our guest speakers.

title: Campus Food Pantry Volunteering
description: Join Alpha Lambda Delta as we give back to our campus community by volunteering at the OU Food Pantry on April 27th. This is a great opportunity to support students in need while engaging in meaningful service alongside fellow ALD members. Volunteers will assist with tasks such as organizing food items, stocking shelves, and helping ensure the pantry remains a welcoming and efficient resource for the OU community. No prior experience is needed—just come ready to help, work as a team, and make a positive impact. Let’s come together to support our community and make a difference!

title: Day of Spring
description: The Baha'i Assosciation is celebrating the Ninth Day of Ridvan, by also celebrating spring! Join us for pot painting, book mark making, and Bouquet Making (Not to mention the free food!) Where: Beaird Lounge OMU Time: 5pm-8pm When: Tuesday, April 28th

title: TBPantry Visit Norman Food & Shelter
description: Come serve with us as a chapter and give back to our community! There will be two shifts: 9 - 10:30 am (Shelf - Stocker) 10:30 - 12:30 (Serving Lunch) Sign up to volunteer HERE!

title: Denim Day Lunch'n Learn
host: Office of Advocacy and Education / OU Food Pantry / OU Higher Education Prevention Services
description: Staff and faculty of the University of Oklahoma are welcome for free entry to our lunch'n learn event! This event is honoring Denim Day, so attendees MUST wear some form of denim to be allowed entry. Food is provided, free of charge. Guest speaker Anna Nasset will be presenting her iconic GET TO WORK: Workplace Culture and Care workshop! (full/part-time and student staff welcome)
